questions.question: Tom has been saving money for a new bike and has $120 in his savings account. He wants to buy a bike that costs $180. If he saves $12 per week, how many weeks will it take him to have enough money to buy the bike? Let $x$ be the number of weeks. The equation is $12x + 120 = 180$. Solve for $x$.

A bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 8 books. If the bookshelf is currently empty and you want to fill it with books, and you have $3x + 2$ books to put on the shelves, how many books will you have left over after filling the bookshelf? The equation is $3x + 2 = 40$. Solve for $x$.

A bakery sells a total of 250 loaves of bread per day. They sell a combination of whole wheat and white bread. If they sell $x$ loaves of whole wheat bread, and $2x + 5$ loaves of white bread, how many loaves of whole wheat bread do they sell if the equation is $x + (2x + 5) = 250$? Solve for $x$.

A group of friends want to rent a party bus that costs $500 plus an additional $10 per person. If they have a budget of $650 and want to know the maximum number of people that can ride the bus, let $x$ be the number of people. Write an equation to represent the situation, solve for $x$, and provide the maximum number of people that can ride the bus. Show all your work and explain your reasoning.

Ashley is saving money to buy a new violin that costs $800. She already has $200 and plans to save $15 per week from her allowance. If she also earns an extra $40 per month from teaching music lessons, how many weeks will it take her to have enough money to buy the violin? Let $x$ be the number of weeks. Write an equation to represent the situation and solve for $x$. Provide the number of weeks it will take Ashley to save enough money.

teacher_rules: Provide clear instructions for each question
Allow students to use a calculator for check-in
Remind students to show their work for the free-response questions
